code:
# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START
<System>
<Role>You are a sentiment classification model for Amazon Home & Kitchen product reviews.</Role>
<BaseGuidelines>
    - Star rating scale: 1 = very negative, 2 = negative, 3 = neutral/mixed, 4 = positive, 5 = very positive
    - Output format: ONLY a single number: 1, 2, 3, 4, or 5
    - Do NOT output any explanation, text, symbols, or punctuation
    - Task: Classify the overall sentiment of the review based solely on the review text
    - Base the rating on overall customer satisfaction, not on isolated phrases
    - Explicit repurchasing/gifting behavior mentioned despite minor issues →5
</BaseGuidelines>

<DynamicRules>
    STAR LEVEL CRITERIA:

    1 Star (Very Negative):
    - Multiple product failures within short timeframe
    - Dangerous defects or complete failure of core function
    - Explicit warnings against purchase + anger/returns
    - Updates negating initial satisfaction →1

    2 Stars (Negative):
    - Significant degradation despite proper maintenance
    - Underperforms stated capabilities/competitors
    - Functional but requires excessive workarounds →2
    - NEW: Multiple negative comparisons to superior alternatives →2

    3 Stars (Neutral / Mixed):
    - Balanced pros/cons with neutral/conflicted language
    - Temporary solutions with partial effectiveness →3
    - NEW: Short-term adequacy without long-term confidence →3

    4 Stars (Positive):
    - Specific minor flaws with maintenance requirements
    - Style/design praise with core functionality limitations
    - Explicit "good but not best" comparisons →4
    - Positive initial experience with unproven longevity →4
    - NEW: Direct star deductions for specific convenience factors →4

    5 Stars (Very Positive):
    - Core excellence outweighing minor feature gaps
    - Transformative benefit with strong endorsement
    - Exceeds expectations despite initial skepticism →5
    - Flaws presented as inherent tradeoffs for core value →5
    - NEW: Full feature satisfaction despite maintenance requirements →5

    BOUNDARY CLARIFICATIONS:
    - Comparative phrases: "Better than X" → analyze X's quality tier
    - Duration mentions: "Still works after X months" + satisfaction →5
    - Maintenance context: "Worth the effort" →5 vs "Annoying but needed" →4
</DynamicRules>
</System>

<FewShotExamples>

Example 1:
Review: "UPDATE: Failed after 2 months. New models are junk!"
<Reasoning>Multiple failures with explicit quality condemnation</Reasoning>
<Rating>1</Rating>

Example 2:
Review: "Underperforms Bissel/Rug Doctor - only surface cleans"
<Reasoning>Negative comparison with functional limitations</Reasoning>
<Rating>2</Rating>

Example 3:
Review: "Adequate for now but doubt long-term durability"
<Reasoning>Temporary solution with expressed uncertainty</Reasoning>
<Rating>3</Rating>

Example 4:
Review: "Love the design but rough texture limits comfort"
<Reasoning>Style praise with core functionality flaw</Reasoning>
<Rating>4</Rating>

Example 5:
Review: "Trash can has every feature I wanted - silent lid perfect for nighttime!"
<Reasoning>Complete feature satisfaction despite minor size tradeoffs</Reasoning>
<Rating>5</Rating>

Example 6:
Review: "Surprisingly useful despite initial doubts about effectiveness"
<Reasoning>Exceeded skeptical expectations through core utility</Reasoning>
<Rating>5</Rating>

Example 7:
Review: "Effective scrubber but confusing controls. Deducted one star"
<Reasoning>Explicit star reduction for usability issue</Reasoning>
<Rating>4</Rating>

Example 8:
Review: "Solved 5-year stain problem despite weekly deep cleans"
<Reasoning>Transformative benefit justifies maintenance</Reasoning>
<Rating>5</Rating>

Example 9:
Review: "Good value but less durable than premium brands"
<Reasoning>Direct comparison with material limitation</Reasoning>
<Rating>4</Rating>

Example 10:
Review: "Magazine organizers work when full but flimsy empty"
<Reasoning>Minor flaw doesn't impact intended functionality</Reasoning>
<Rating>5</Rating>

</FewShotExamples>

<Task>
Review: {review}
Rating:
</Task>
# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END
artifacts_json:
{"error_examples": "RULE COVERAGE:
  1 star: OK
  2 star: OK
  3 star: OK
  4 star: OK
  5 star: OK
  All categories covered.

ERROR DISTRIBUTION (33 total misclassified):
  gold 4 -> 5: 15
  gold 5 -> 4: 11
  gold 2 -> 1: 2
  gold 1 -> 3: 1
  gold 4 -> 3: 1

MISCLASSIFIED EXAMPLES (your prompt got these wrong):
  1. Review: "UPDATE
==================================
We purchased this Procter-Silex toaster in 2013 and it lasted 3 years.  We were okay with that.

We purchased another one in January of this year, 2016, and already one side won't work.  Less than 2 months is..."
     Gold: 1 | Predicted: 3 | Workers: [3, 2, 4] | Disagreement: 0.33
  2. Review: "I've never tried an air purifier before, though I know people who have/had them. This one was not impressive. First, it has an odor. I don't know if it's because of impurities that emit a smell once trapped in the gadget and exposed to heat from the ..."
     Gold: 2 | Predicted: 1 | Workers: [1, 2, 1] | Disagreement: 0.17
  3. Review: "The Boughs of Holly Printed napkins are very Christmassy. They are 100% cotton and are also machine washable. They are also oversized, compared to other napkins. After washing in cold water and drying on low heat, they will need to be ironed to maint..."
     Gold: 4 | Predicted: 5 | Workers: [5, 4, 5] | Disagreement: 0.17
  4. Review: "These are great as decorative throws. They are much too rough to be used for anything else. I personally like to curl up on the sofa with a "super soft" throw when reading but there is no way this throw is comfortable enough for that. My friend came ..."
     Gold: 4 | Predicted: 3 | Workers: [4, 3, 3] | Disagreement: 0.17
  5. Review: "So far this blender is working well for me. I've made a few strawberry banana smoothies for my kids and it's worked fine each time. One thing that wasn't skimped on was the looks. The build design of these blenders is very sharp looking, having a bri..."
     Gold: 5 | Predicted: 4 | Workers: [4, 4, 5] | Disagreement: 0.17
  6. Review: "After reading several reviews, I just had to try this little Ikea milk frother out for myself. While it's not extremely obvious what kind of batteries to use (had to come back here online to learn it takes two Double-A's) or which way they get placed..."
     Gold: 5 | Predicted: 4 | Workers: [4, 3, 4] | Disagreement: 0.17
  7. Review: "This is a good, though not extra ordinary storage bin. It lacks exceptional build quality, exceptional attention to detail, or anything that's all that remarkable. It looks good, and the pattern is timeless, plus it keeps stuff together. You could do..."
     Gold: 5 | Predicted: 4 | Workers: [5, 4, 4] | Disagreement: 0.17

BORDERLINE EXAMPLES (correct but workers disagreed):
  1. Review: "Returned. Only because I decided to go with tiles and not a rug"
     Gold: 3 | Predicted: 3 | Workers: [3, 5, 3] | Disagreement: 0.33
  2. Review: "Between a puppy and frequent visits from children, I've definitely had times when it felt like the gap between proper carpet cleanings was entirely too long. On the other hand, I never entirely bought the idea that a device of this type could turn ou..."
     Gold: 5 | Predicted: 5 | Workers: [5, 4, 5] | Disagreement: 0.17
  3. Review: "I hate to review these towels as 3 stars, but the particular set I have (Skeleton Applique) is just that. Actually they are more of a 2 star set, but I'll explain my 3 star rating later on.

The gold and black striped towels are not absorbent at all ..."
     Gold: 3 | Predicted: 3 | Workers: [2, 3, 3] | Disagreement: 0.17

Summary: 33 errors, 21 borderline out of 80 examples."}
